content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 2, 1987

Honorable Nick Kinney
Kings County Supervisor
1400 W. Lacey Blvd.
Hanford, CA 93230

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-87-274

Dear Mr. Kinney:

We have received your letter requesting advice concerning
your duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Does a campaign contribution which you received from an oil
company require you to disqualify yourself from voting on an
assessment appeal by the oil company which is before the county
board of equalization?

CONCLUSION

The campaign contribution is not a basis for your
disqualification from the decision.

FACTS

You received a $250.00 campaign contribution from Beacon
Oil Company when you ran for the Kings County Board of
Supervisors. You were elected to the board.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative
Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are
to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Honorable Nick Kinney
November 24, 1987
Page 2


     Beacon now has an assessment appeal before the Kings County
Board of Equalization.  The board of supervisors sits as the
board of equalization.

                              ANALYSIS

     The campaign contribution disqualification provisions of
the Act are found in Section 84308.  Section 84308(a)(3)
provides that local government agencies whose members are
directly elected by the voters are not covered by Section
84308.  Regulation 18438.1 further provides:

     (a)  The officers of an agency exempted by
Government Code Section 84308(a)(3) are exempted only
when:

          (1)  They are acting as members of the
     governing body of the agency, and the body is
     acting in its entirety as itself or as the
     ex officio governing body of any other agency.

     Thus, because the board of supervisors sits in its
entirety as the board of equalization, the board of
equalization is exempt from the coverage of Section
84308.  Accordingly, you need not disqualify from the
board of equalization's decision involving Beacon Oil.

     If you have any further questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: John G. McLean
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:JGM:mek

NICK KINNEY
Supervisor
District 4

BOARD OF SUPERVISORS
KingsCounty Government Center
1400 W. Lacey Boulevard
Hanford, California 93230
Phone (209) 582-3211 - Ext. 2367

October 27, 1987

Robert E. Leidigh, Counsel
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California   95804-0807

Re:  Request for Opinion on Conflict of Interest

Gentlemen:

A question has been asked whether I must disqualify myself
from voting on the assessment appeal by Beacon Oil Company, Inc.,
which is currently scheduled before the Kings County Board of
Equalization.  The Board of Supervisors sits as the Kings County
Board of Equalization.

I am a duly elected Supervisor of Kings County.  I came onto
the board on January 1, 1987.  My term of office expires in
January, 1991.  My home address is 1044 N. Williams, Hanford,
California  93230, telephone number (209) 582-7472.  I may also
be reached at the Kings County Government Center, Hanford,
California  93230, telephone (209) 582-3211, extention 2367.

Beacon Oil has its refinery and central offices in Hanford,
California.  On September 15, 1987, Beacon Oil filed for an
assessment  appeal  before  the  Kings  County  Board  of
Equalization.  The hearing on the appeal is not currently
scheduled but could be heard as early as December 1987.

I received a $250.00 campaign contribution from Beacon Oil
Company on or about October 3, 1986, when I ran for election for
Kings County Board of Supervisors.  The contribution was made and
reported properly, in conformance with the laws governing
campaign contributions.

There was an assessment appeal by Beacon Oil Company pending
before the Board of Equalization at the time of the campaign
contribution.  However, I was not then on the Board of

Robert E. Leigh, Counsel
October 27, 1987
Page Two


Supervisors. I have no business or financial relationship of any
kind with Beacon Oil Company. The question is whether this
single contribution would disqualify me or limit my participation
in the assessment appeal proceedings before the Kings County
Board of Equalization.

I would very much appreciate an opinion on the issue raised.

Very truly yours,

NICK KINNEY
Supervisor, District IV
